observations.statement: The documented endpoint is POST /api/v2/awards/funding/ and is described as listing federal account financial data for a requested award for the Award V2 summary page Federal Account Funding tab.
The documented request body contains required award_id plus optional limit, page, sort, and order fields.
Documentation says generated award identifiers are preferred while numeric surrogate award ids remain accepted for backward compatibility.

source.title: Staged docs response schema for Award Funding

observations.statement: The documented response is a JSON object with results array and page_metadata object.

Documentation lists funding records with reporting fiscal period fields, quarterly/monthly submission flag, federal account and title, funding and awarding agency identifiers and names, program activity, object class, disaster_emergency_fund_code, transaction_obligated_amount, and gross_outlay_amount.
Documentation describes pagination metadata fields page, next, previous, hasNext, and hasPrevious.

The current raw profile describes the endpoint as returning federal account funding records for a given award, including obligated and outlay amounts by reporting period, and records prior live observations that the route was callable without authentication.
The current raw profile records prior live behavior that unknown award ids return HTTP 200 with an empty results array and that the endpoint does not expose a total record count for pagination.

The current raw profile records prior live behavior that the documented default sort differs from actual behavior and that disaster_emergency_fund_code values are returned as letter codes or null instead of booleans.

source.title: Derived request coverage check against current raw profile

observations.statement: The current raw profile exposes all five documented top-level request fields: award_id, limit, page, sort, and order; no additional important top-level request field omissions were found from the staged docs.

The major remaining gaps are semantic rather than top-level request-field coverage: doc/profile disagreement on default sort, undocumented meaning of disaster_emergency_fund_code letters, and lack of total-count pagination metadata.

source.title: Live probe of baseline award funding page

results.account_title: Salaries and Expenses, Social Security Administration
Salaries and Expenses, Social Security Administration

observations.statement: A live POST with award_id and limit returned HTTP 200 JSON, confirming the endpoint was callable on 2026-05-10.

The live response matched the documented top-level shape with results and page_metadata and included the documented funding-row fields such as reporting fiscal period, agencies, federal_account, account_title, program activity, object class, transaction_obligated_amount, and gross_outlay_amount.
The live sample contradicts the documented boolean type for disaster_emergency_fund_code because the response returned null in one row and the string code 'Q' in another row.

With sort omitted, the live sample returned account_title 'Salaries and Expenses, Social Security Administration' rows before 'Office of the Inspector General, Social Security Administration' rows, and within the same account the 2017 row appeared before the 2022 row, contradicting the documented default of reporting_fiscal_date descending.

source.title: Reviewer-confirmed awards funding safe-template page/order call

observations.statement: The reviewer reported that validateRequest warned page and order were still marked documented_unverified before repair, even though the safe-template request shape itself was otherwise accepted.
The reviewer reported a successful bounded call with body {award_id:"CONT_AWD_0002_2800_SS001740003_2800", page:1, limit:4, sort:"reporting_fiscal_date", order:"desc"} that returned HTTP 200.

The same reviewer-confirmed response included page_metadata with page 1, next null, previous null, hasNext false, and hasPrevious false, confirming that page=1 was accepted and echoed in the response.

source.title: Reviewer report for award funding canonical award lookup alias
source.locator: Structured reviewer report selecting repair-2-canonical-award-id-alias for v2__awards__funding

observations.statement: The reviewer-backed drilldown selected a search row with display Award ID HT940216C0001 and generated_internal_id CONT_AWD_HT940216C0001_9700_-NONE-_-NONE-, then used that generated value successfully against POST /api/v2/awards/funding/ under request body field award_id.
The reviewer reported that award-detail guidance already treats generated_internal_id from spending_by_award as safer than the human-facing display Award ID for follow-up lookup.

source.title: Reviewer report for award funding canonical award lookup alias
source.locator: Structured reviewer report selecting repair-2-canonical-award-id-alias for v2__awards__funding

observations.statement: The same generated award identifier carried through search, award detail, and award funding even though the related endpoints label that business key differently.

In the reviewer-backed workflow, v2__awards__funding accepted the search-generated hash under request field name award_id, while v2__awards__award_id discusses or echoes the same business key as generated_unique_award_id.

source.title: Reviewer report selecting repair-3-funding-reconciliation-and-validation for v2__awards__funding
source.locator: Structured reviewer report selecting repair-3-funding-reconciliation-and-validation for v2__awards__funding

observations.statement: In the reviewer-backed HT940216C0001 drilldown, award detail reported total_obligation 51269205263.03 and total_account_obligation 321840 for the same generated award identifier carried into funding.

For that same generated identifier, the funding endpoint returned exactly one row with transaction_obligated_amount 321840, gross_outlay_amount null, reporting_fiscal_year 2020, reporting_fiscal_quarter 4, reporting_fiscal_month 12, and page_metadata.hasNext false.

The reviewer reported that validateRequest warned on sort even though the request used the documented safe explicit pattern sort=reporting_fiscal_date and order=desc and the live call succeeded, so the contradiction is in omitted default behavior rather than in explicit documented sort values.

source.title: Reviewer report selecting RT-2 explicit-sort safety and DEFC raw-code typing for v2__awards__funding
source.locator: Structured reviewer report selecting RT-2 for v2__awards__funding

observations.statement: The selected RT-2 reviewer workflow treated the explicit-sort safe template as the primary request pattern: body {award_id:"CONT_AWD_HT940216C0001_9700_-NONE-_-NONE-", page:1, limit:10, sort:"reporting_fiscal_date", order:"desc"} validated cleanly and the live funding call succeeded.

The same RT-2 reviewer report said omitted-sort behavior is contradicted by the endpoint's known caveats, so deterministic pagination and comparisons should use explicit sort plus order rather than relying on documented defaults.

source.title: Reviewer report selecting RT-2 explicit-sort safety and DEFC raw-code typing for v2__awards__funding
source.locator: Structured reviewer report selecting RT-2 for v2__awards__funding

observations.statement: In the RT-2 reviewer's explicit-sort funding drilldown for CONT_AWD_HT940216C0001_9700_-NONE-_-NONE-, the single returned row had disaster_emergency_fund_code "Q" and gross_outlay_amount null with hasNext false.
That reviewer evidence confirms disaster_emergency_fund_code should be modeled as a raw code/string-or-null field rather than a boolean-like flag.
